summary: D14: deploy/vps-bootstrap.sh — one-time setup from §7.4 as runnable script (includes Ollama install + model pull per B6c)
description:
One-time setup from §7.4 as a runnable script. Version-controlled because you want it on incident rebuilds.

BASE OS: Debian 13 (trixie). Original spec said Ubuntu 24.04; updated in commit TBD. Debian-specific notes:
- postgresql-17-pgvector: prefer the PGDG APT repo on both Debian and Ubuntu; Debian main's copy lags.
- caddy: needs Caddy's official APT repo (cloudsmith), same dance on both distros.
- Other packages (ufw, fail2ban, unattended-upgrades, NodeSource node, Ollama installer, useradd --system, /usr/sbin/nologin, dpkg-reconfigure) are identical Debian/Ubuntu — no adjustments.

ADDITIONS (per B6c, commit todo:77272cb5):
- Install Ollama: curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
- Pull embedding model: ollama pull nomic-embed-text:v1.5
- Disable Ollama's default systemd unit (binds 0.0.0.0:11434), install
  deploy/ollama.service instead (binds 127.0.0.1:11434), enable + start it.
- Verify: curl -s http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags | grep nomic-embed-text:v1.5

resolution.note: deploy/vps-bootstrap.sh written. Debian 13 trixie target per updated spec; Tailscale-only SSH per vps_access_tailscale memory (no HOME_IP rules); Ollama loopback per B6c; PGDG + Caddy APT repos; idempotent with per-step guards. /etc/guru-bootstrap.env holds DOMAIN, TS_AUTHKEY, optional DEPLOY_PUBKEY. Not tested end-to-end — user will run on the prod VPS when ready; feedback_db_safety prevents local run during active pipeline fill.
